decision.archive_summary: The conversation iterated on Codex context minor GC regression coverage. Earlier work added repeatable smoke/eval cases and harder Chinese multi-turn detour cases into fixed regressions, then tightened the packaged guard to avoid whitelist-like hardcoding and to respect shadow-guard vetoes. By the end, the active thread narrowed to whether the previously failing test should now pass and to running it automatically.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest user turn asks to run the previously discussed test, so this is the same active topic: relation=continue. The immediately relevant raw dependency is the recent exchange establishing that the once-failing test is now expected to pass and then reporting it was run, so those turns should remain raw. Older implementation history about adding earlier cases is resolved background and can leave the next-turn prompt. The anti-whitelist/guard-tightening point is still potentially relevant as durable context for why the fix should be trusted, so preserve it as compact semantic pins rather than raw turns.
